title: Obsolete ~1759-line legacy engine body (runWorkflowBodyLegacy) is unreachable in production
evidence: runWorkflowBody only calls runWorkflowBodyLegacy when opts.__smithersEngineMode === 'legacy' || process.env.SMITHERS_LEGACY_ENGINE === '1'. Grep shows neither flag is set anywhere in apps/cli, packages/smithers, or .smithers — only 3 engine test files set it. .smithers/specs/migration-plan.md:247/254 explicitly describes SMITHERS_LEGACY_ENGINE as a temporary rollback flag to 'Remove ... after 2 weeks of green CI'. The driver path is the default and the legacy body shares only 358 of its 1759 lines with the driver body, so it is a parallel, diverging implementation maintained solely for its own tests. (Note: the separate legacyExecuteTask at line 2594 is NOT dead — it is the agent-execution path used by the live driver body at 5068.)
recommendation: Delete runWorkflowBodyLegacy, the __smithersEngineMode/SMITHERS_LEGACY_ENGINE gate, and the three legacy-only tests (engine-legacy-mode.test.jsx and the legacy variants in parallel-loop-advancement/aspects-budget). Keep legacyExecuteTask. This removes ~1759 lines of obsolete code per the completed migration plan.
vnote: Substance confirmed. engine.js:4624-4630 gates runWorkflowBodyLegacy behind opts.__smithersEngineMode==='legacy' || process.env.SMITHERS_LEGACY_ENGINE==='1', else returns runWorkflowBodyDriver (the default). Grepped the entire repo (apps/, packages/, .smithers/, .github/workflows/): neither flag is set in any production code or CI config — only 3 engine test files set it (aspects-budget.test.jsx:156, parallel-loop-advancement.test.jsx:79, engine-legacy-mode.test.jsx:18/28). The legacy body spans 5829-7587 (~1758 lines, computed from the next top-level function at 7588); the driver body 4679-5822 (~1143 lines) — two separate large implementations. migration-plan.md:247/254 confirms SMITHERS_LEGACY_ENGINE is a temporary rollback flag to 'Remove ... after 2 weeks of green CI'. One nuance: strictly it is not unreachable 'dead code' — it is still reachable via the test-only flags and the 3 legacy tests still execute it in CI, so it is better characterized as an obsolete parallel implementation maintained solely for its own tests per a completed migration. Severity P1 and the dead-code/cleanup intent are sound.

title: deferred-bridge.js is entirely dead code (non-durable bridge superseded by durable variant)
evidence: Every export (DeferredBridgeWorkflow, makeApprovalDeferred, makeTimerDeferred, makeDeferredBridgeKey, bridgeApprovalResolve, bridgeTimerResolve, getDeferredResolution) is imported nowhere. index.js:34 even documents 'deferred-bridge.js is intentionally not re-exported'. Grep across packages/apps/.smithers for each symbol returns only the file itself plus a comment in index.js. The live code path is durable-deferred-bridge.js. The supporting .ts type stubs ApprovalDeferredResolution.ts / DeferredResolution.ts exist only for this dead file.
recommendation: Delete src/effect/deferred-bridge.js and its two type stubs (ApprovalDeferredResolution.ts, DeferredResolution.ts).
vnote: Confirmed. Grepped the whole repo: none of deferred-bridge.js's 7 exports (DeferredBridgeWorkflow, makeApprovalDeferred, makeTimerDeferred, makeDeferredBridgeKey, bridgeApprovalResolve, bridgeTimerResolve, getDeferredResolution) are imported anywhere. The many bridgeApprovalResolve hits all resolve to the DIFFERENT durable-deferred-bridge.js export (5-arg signature with adapter), not the 4-arg deferred-bridge.js one. index.js:34 documents 'deferred-bridge.js is intentionally not re-exported'. The type stubs ApprovalDeferredResolution.ts and DeferredResolution.ts form a closed island used only by this dead file (DeferredResolution.ts imports ApprovalDeferredResolution.ts; both referenced only from deferred-bridge.js); neither appears in index.d.ts/index.js. Recommendation to delete the file plus the 2 stubs is correct.

title: Dead exports in durable-deferred-bridge.js (Workflow + success schemas never consumed)
evidence: Grep for these three symbols across packages/apps/.smithers returns only the definition site. They are not re-exported by workflow-bridge.js and not imported anywhere. Additionally makeApprovalDurableDeferred/makeWaitForEventDurableDeferred (re-exported publicly) produce DurableDeferred values that are only ever passed as the unused _deferred parameter of awaitBridgeDeferred/resolveBridgeDeferred (lines 173, 187) — the actual resolution uses an in-memory deferredResolutions Map keyed by executionId, so the DurableDeferred.make() calls are pure overhead.
recommendation: Delete the three unused exports. Either drop the makeApproval/WaitForEventDurableDeferred indirection or stop passing the unused _deferred argument, since the @effect/workflow DurableDeferred machinery is not actually driving resolution here.
vnote: Confirmed both parts. (1) DurableDeferredBridgeWorkflow (line 19), approvalDurableDeferredSuccessSchema (44), waitForEventDurableDeferredSuccessSchema (51) have zero references outside their definition file — not re-exported by workflow-bridge.js (which only re-exports bridge*Resolve/await*/make*/makeDurableDeferredBridgeExecutionId at line 20) and absent from index.d.ts. (2) Verified by reading lines 166-189: awaitBridgeDeferred(executionId,_deferred) and resolveBridgeDeferred(executionId,_deferred,exit) ignore the _deferred argument entirely and use the in-memory deferredResolutions Map keyed by executionId. makeApprovalDurableDeferred/makeWaitForEventDurableDeferred call DurableDeferred.make() purely to feed the unused _deferred parameter, so the @effect/workflow DurableDeferred machinery is genuine overhead that drives nothing.

title: rpc-schema.js (SmithersRpcGroup + payload/result schemas) is published but has no implementation or consumer
evidence: Exported via index.js:36 (export * from './effect/rpc-schema.js'). Grep for SmithersRpcGroup, ApprovalPayloadSchema, RunSummarySchema, ListRunsPayloadSchema, GetRunResultSchema across the whole repo returns only the definition site and its own .ts type stubs. No server mounts SmithersRpcGroup and no client uses it. The workflow-bridge.js header still calls the bridge a 'Phase 0 Seam Adapter' — this looks like leftover scaffolding from an unfinished RPC phase. It has zero tests.
recommendation: Either wire SmithersRpcGroup into the real CLI/gateway RPC surface (and add a contract test), or remove rpc-schema.js and its 11 .ts type stubs from the published surface.
vnote: Confirmed. rpc-schema.js is re-exported via index.js:36 (export * from './effect/rpc-schema.js') and surfaces in index.d.ts (SmithersRpcGroup at 1559, the schemas at 1472/1505/1509/1533, plus the big export list at 1685). Grepped the repo: SmithersRpcGroup / ApprovalPayloadSchema / RunSummarySchema / ListRunsPayloadSchema / GetRunResultSchema have no consumer — no server mounts the group, no client calls it. The only other @effect/rpc usages (sandbox-entity.js, entity-worker.js) do not import rpc-schema.js. No test references rpc-schema. Medium confidence is appropriate since it could be intentional published SDK surface, but as shipped it is unwired scaffolding.

title: AlertRuntime is a no-op stub; alertPolicy.rules are never evaluated and no alert is ever inserted
evidence: AlertRuntime.start()/stop() are empty method bodies ({}) yet engine.js constructs it with full services (requestCancel, createHumanRequest, pauseScheduler) and calls .start() whenever effectiveAlertPolicy.rules is non-empty. The db exposes insertAlert/listAlerts and the CLI lists alerts (apps/cli/src/index.js:4487), but grep shows insertAlert is never called anywhere in engine or CLI. So a workflow configured with an alertPolicy silently does nothing — the runtime that should evaluate rules against events and raise alerts/human-requests is an empty shell.
recommendation: Implement AlertRuntime (subscribe to the eventBus, evaluate policy.rules, insertAlert / createHumanRequest / requestCancel per rule action) with unit tests for each rule outcome, or remove the alertPolicy surface if unsupported. The pauseScheduler service is also stubbed to () => {} at the engine call site, reinforcing that the feature is incomplete.
vnote: Confirmed and arguably stronger than stated. alert-runtime.js:20-21 has start(){} and stop(){} as literal no-ops. engine.js:5488-5514 (and 6059-6088 in the legacy body) construct AlertRuntime with full services (requestCancel, createHumanRequest, pauseScheduler) when effectiveAlertPolicy.rules is non-empty and call .start(), which does nothing. insertAlert is defined in packages/db (adapter.js:1713) and tested only in packages/db/tests/alert-instances.test.js — never called from engine or CLI. The CLI exposes 'alerts list|ack|resolve|silence' (apps/cli/src/index.js:4463). The full SmithersAlertPolicy surface (rules with afterMs/reaction/severity/owner/runbook/labels, plus reactions) is defined in scheduler/SmithersWorkflowOptions.ts:21-37 but NOTHING consumes rules OR reactions (grep for reaction usage in engine/CLI returns nothing). pauseScheduler is wired as (_reason)=>{}. There is even a test (engine-small-utils.test.js:27) that asserts start()/stop() are no-ops, codifying the unimplemented state. A configured alertPolicy silently does nothing. P1 missing-feature confirmed.

title: AgentTraceCollector (716 lines, ~28 methods) has a single direct unit test; persistence/error/lifecycle paths untested
evidence: The one unit test exercises processStructuredStdoutLine / observeProviderSessionRow / final-text handling. Untested by name: flush(), persistNdjson(), rewriteNdjson(), importProviderSessionTranscript(), resolveProviderSessionFile(), observeError(), observeResult(), resolveCompleteness(), onStdout/onStderr buffering, emitObservedBatch(), begin()/endListener() lifecycle. cli-agent-event-output-capture.test.jsx is an engine integration regression test, not a collector unit test. This is the most complex stateful class in the package and its NDJSON persistence + provider-session import + completeness/error branches are not covered.
recommendation: Add a focused unit suite for AgentTraceCollector covering NDJSON persist/rewrite (with a temp logDir), provider session file resolution + transcript import, observeError/observeResult, completeness resolution, and the begin/endListener subscription lifecycle.
vnote: Largely confirmed with one nuance. The only direct unit test (agent-trace-collector-structured.test.js, 1 case) exercises processStructuredStdoutLine, observeProviderSessionRow, emitObservedBatch, finalText. All the named methods exist (begin:156, endListener:161, onStdout:167, onStderr:172, observeResult:177, observeError:196, flush:202, resolveCompleteness:450, importProviderSessionTranscript:649, resolveProviderSessionFile:670, persistNdjson:687, rewriteNdjson:710). cli-agent-event-output-capture.test.jsx asserts only the output row, not collector behavior. NUANCE: engine.js DOES call begin()/observeError()/observeResult()/flush() during real task execution (3413/3533-3541), and flush() internally calls importProviderSessionTranscript/resolveCompleteness/persistNdjson/rewriteNdjson — so the ~10 engine tests that run CLI agents give these methods incidental LINE coverage. But none assert on NDJSON file contents, provider-session resolution/import, completeness outcomes, or observeError branches. So 'untested' is too strong (they execute), but 'no focused behavioral coverage of the package's most complex stateful class' is accurate. P1 is defensible given the persistence/import/completeness branch surface; the recommendation for a dedicated unit suite stands.

title: optimization-artifact.js has zero direct unit tests despite being on the production render path
evidence: Used in production at engine.js:4430/5584 (applyOptimizationArtifactToTasks) and the env var via apps/cli/src/optimize-command.js. Grep of packages/engine/tests for loadOptimizationArtifact / applyOptimizationArtifactToTasks / promptPatches returns nothing. Untested branches: env-default path, mtime cache hit vs miss, INVALID_INPUT for missing file and for non-object JSON, promptPatches-vs-patches fallback, and the per-task application guard (skip when no patch / no task.agent / non-string prompt) plus the optimizationArtifactId/strategy meta merge.
recommendation: Add a unit test for optimization-artifact.js covering the cache, both error paths, the promptPatches/patches fallback, and applyOptimizationArtifactToTasks selective patching + meta annotation.

title: task-compute-fns.js (subflow/sandbox compute-fn attachment) has zero direct unit tests
evidence: Both functions are used in production at engine.js:4431/4435/5586/5590/6670/6674. Grep of tests for attachSubflowComputeFns / attachSandboxComputeFns / __subflow / __sandbox finds no direct test. The functions mutate task.meta (stripping __subflowWorkflow/__sandboxWorkflow before persistence) and install computeFns that throw WORKFLOW_EXECUTION_FAILED on non-finished child status — none of this guard/strip logic is unit-covered.
recommendation: Add unit tests asserting computeFn attachment, the meta-stripping of non-persistable keys, the skip conditions (existing computeFn / missing __subflowWorkflow), and the non-finished-status failure path.

title: EventBus.persistDb advances this.seq even when the DB assigns its own seq, leaving this.seq as misleading dead state
evidence: Line 138 builds eventRow with seq: this.seq++. But lines 140-145 prefer insertEventWithNextSeqEffect/insertEventWithNextSeq, which take nextSeqRow (no seq) and let the DB compute the next seq. SmithersDb exposes insertEventWithNextSeqEffect (db/adapter.js:2658), so in production the eventRow with the incremented seq is never used while this.seq still advances. startSeq is seeded from getLastEventSeq (engine.js:4711) but this.seq is never read back for any DB write on the production path, so it can silently drift from the real DB seq.
recommendation: Only increment this.seq on the branch that actually uses eventRow.seq (the insertEvent/insertEventEffect fallback), or drop the eventRow/this.seq bookkeeping entirely since the adapter owns sequence assignment.

title: builder fragment(_inputSchema) silently discards its input schema argument
evidence: fragment(_inputSchema) is documented to 'Build a graph fragment' taking an AnySchema, but the body is `return makeFactory();` — the schema is never passed to makeFactory or attached/validated anywhere. Tests (effect-builder.test.js:225-253) pass an inputSchema but only assert handle uniqueness, not schema enforcement. fragment is part of the published API (re-exported from smithers-orchestrator). Callers relying on the declared input schema get no validation.
recommendation: Either thread _inputSchema into makeFactory for input typing/validation, or change the signature/docs to fragment() so the API doesn't imply schema enforcement it doesn't provide.
vnote: Confirmed. builder.js:1201-1203 fragment(_inputSchema) body is exactly 'return makeFactory();'. makeFactory() (line 928) takes no parameters, so _inputSchema is dropped — never threaded, attached, or validated. It is published API: re-exported on the Smithers object (builder.js:1237) and declared in index.d.ts:1307 as fragment(_inputSchema: AnySchema$1). The docs actively advertise the schema arg: apps/cli/docs/llms-full.txt:9847-9850 show Smithers.fragment(diffInputSchema) with steps doing run:({input})=>readDiff(input.path), implying the input schema is meaningful — but it is not enforced. Tests (effect-builder.test.js:227/235/248) pass inputSchema yet only assert handle uniqueness/constructor presence, never schema enforcement. Real API/docs mismatch; P2 bug confirmed.

title: subscribeTaskWorkerDispatches is a published observability hook with no production consumer
evidence: Grep across packages/apps/.smithers shows the only callers are two engine test files (entity-worker-contract.test.js, single-runner-internals.test.js). No CLI, server, or .smithers code subscribes to worker dispatches. It is exported publicly but unused outside its own tests.
recommendation: Confirm whether this is intended public SDK surface; if not, remove it. If kept, document the intended consumer.
vnote: Confirmed. Defined single-runner.js:191, re-exported via workflow-bridge.js:28 and surfaced in index.d.ts:1075 + the public export list (1685). Grepped the whole repo: the only callers are two engine test files (entity-worker-contract.test.js:9/72, single-runner-internals.test.js:6/27/28). No CLI, server, or .smithers code subscribes. It exists as published SDK surface used only by its own tests (which verify the engine notifies dispatch subscribers via __singleRunnerInternals.notifyDispatchSubscribers). Medium confidence appropriate since it may be intended public observability surface; if so it lacks a documented consumer.

title: Stale 'Phase 0 Seam Adapter' doc comment claims the bridge will be replaced by Activity.make()
evidence: Header comment: 'Currently, it delegates to the legacy implementations exactly as they are. In Phase 1, executeTaskBridge will be replaced by Activity.make(). In subsequent phases, other engine boundaries will be modeled as Workflows.' executeTaskBridge is the production bridge today (uses executeTaskActivity + the compute/static bridges + legacyExecuteTask), not a placeholder. The aspirational phasing language is misleading for maintainers.
recommendation: Update the header to describe the bridge's actual current responsibility, or drop the Phase 0/1 framing now that the driver migration is complete.

title: Dead boolean condition in extractBalancedJson (c === '"' && !escape) — escape is always false at that point
evidence: Lines 16-23 return/continue whenever escape is true, so by line 24 escape is always false; the `&& !escape` guard can never be false and is dead. extractBalancedJson is used internally by engine.js (3646/3666/3686/3793) and indirectly tested only via extractLastBalancedJson (json-extraction.test.js imports only the Last variant), so the direct function lacks its own test for the in-string-brace and unterminated cases.
recommendation: Remove the redundant !escape guard and add a direct unit test for extractBalancedJson covering braces inside strings, escaped quotes, and unbalanced/no-brace inputs.

title: Confusing snapshot-handle defaults: public snapshot() hardcodes source 'watch'/tier 2 while its comment says Tier 1/wrap
evidence: The returned snapshot(req) is commented 'Tier 1 entry point for the in-process tool wrap (Phase 2) and CLI hooks (Phase 3)' but its defaults are source: 'watch', tier: 2. It works only because the engine caller (engine.js:3452) spreads { source: 'wrap', tier: 1 } last to override. Also the StartDurabilityOptions typedef (44-57) omits withSocket and createSocketServer even though both are destructured at 77-78.
recommendation: Make the public snapshot() default to source 'wrap'/tier 1 to match its documented purpose (callers can still override), and document withSocket/createSocketServer in the typedef.

title: alertPolicy.reactions are never consumed anywhere (entire alert reaction pipeline unimplemented)
evidence: SmithersAlertPolicy (packages/scheduler/src/SmithersWorkflowOptions.ts:33-37) defines reactions?: Record<string, SmithersAlertReaction> and rules carry reaction?: SmithersAlertReactionRef (line 30). Grepping engine/src and apps/cli/src for any 'reaction' usage returns nothing — not just rules but the reactions map is dead config. This is part of the same unimplemented alert feature as the AlertRuntime no-op, confirming the whole policy.reactions/rules evaluation surface is non-functional, not merely the rule loop.
recommendation: (verifier-discovered)

title: rawQuery's read-only SQL guard (validateReadOnlyRawQuery) has zero tests anywhere in the repo
evidence: packages/db/src/adapter.js:158-197 implements a security-sensitive validator (stripSqlCommentsAndLiterals, RAW_QUERY_FORBIDDEN_KEYWORDS = drop/delete/insert/update/alter/create/attach/detach/pragma, single-statement enforcement via semicolon detection, RAW_QUERY_ALLOWED_PREFIX). adapter.rawQuery is reachable in production (packages/scorers/src/aggregate.js:42,52) and exposed via the gateway. grep across packages/*/tests, apps/*/tests, e2e for rawQuery/validateReadOnlyRawQuery/stripSqlCommentsAndLiterals returns 0 matches.
recommendation: Add unit tests for validateReadOnlyRawQuery covering: empty/whitespace query, comment-only query, forbidden keyword in each form (incl. keyword hidden in a string literal that should be allowed after stripping), multi-statement rejection, allowed prefixes (select/with/explain/values), and a happy-path rawQuery round-trip against an in-memory DB.
vnote: Verified adapter.js:94-197 implements stripSqlCommentsAndLiterals + validateReadOnlyRawQuery (forbidden keywords drop/delete/insert/update/alter/create/attach/detach/pragma, single-statement check, allowed prefix select|with|explain|values). adapter.rawQuery (adapter.js:596-615) calls it. Repo-wide grep: rawQuery appears in packages/scorers (src/aggregate.js:42,52 production; scorers-aggregate.test.js where it is MOCKED with `rawQuery: async (sql) => {...}`) and StorageServiceShape.ts/InMemoryStorage.js (the latter returns Effect.succeed([])). No db test calls adapter.rawQuery; grep for validateReadOnlyRawQuery/stripSqlCommentsAndLiterals across all of packages/apps/e2e/.smithers/scripts returns only the definitions in adapter.js. The security-sensitive validator and the rawQuery round-trip are entirely untested.

title: Entire in-memory storage module (storage/) is dead code
evidence: src/storage/InMemoryStorage.js (485 lines, makeInMemoryStorageService + InMemoryStorageLive), src/storage/StorageService.js (Context.Tag), and src/storage/StorageServiceShape.ts form a complete alternate StorageService implementation. It is NOT exported from src/index.js, and grep for StorageService/InMemoryStorage/makeInMemoryStorageService/InMemoryStorageLive/db/storage across packages, apps, .smithers, scripts (excluding src/storage) finds only doc-spec references — no importers. The ./* export could expose db/storage/* but nobody imports it.
recommendation: Delete the storage/ module, or wire it up if an in-memory backend is intended (e.g. for tests). As-is it is a 600+ line maintenance liability that drifts from the real adapter (see the answerHumanRequest arg-order bug below).
vnote: src/storage/ = InMemoryStorage.js (484 lines), StorageService.js (Context.Tag, 7 lines), StorageServiceShape.ts (122), StorageServiceTypes.ts (150) = 763 lines total. Not exported from index.js (no storage re-export) and absent from index.d.ts (grep count 0). Repo-wide grep for StorageService/InMemoryStorage/makeInMemoryStorageService/InMemoryStorageLive/StorageServiceTypes outside src/storage finds ONLY unrelated Effect doc text in .smithers/specs/effect-core-library.md (a generic Effect Persistence example, not an import). No db/storage subpath import anywhere. Zero test references. Finding understated size (763 vs stated 600+).

title: Parallel duplicate implementations: output/, frame-codec/, internal-schema/index.js, loadInputEffect.js, loadOutputsEffect.js
evidence: The barrel index.js imports the single-file canonical versions (output.js, frame-codec.js, internal-schema.js, snapshot.js). The directory variants duplicate them: src/output/{buildOutputRow,getKeyColumns,selectOutputRowEffect,upsertOutputRowEffect,validateOutput,validateExistingOutput,getAgentOutputSchema,describeSchemaShape,stripAutoColumns,buildKeyWhere,index}.js and src/frame-codec/{encodeFrameDelta,applyFrameDelta,applyFrameDeltaJson,parseFrameDelta,serializeFrameDelta,normalizeFrameEncoding,FRAME_KEYFRAME_INTERVAL,index}.js are reachable only via db/output/* and db/frame-codec/* subpaths, which grep shows nobody imports (consumers use @smithers-orchestrator/db/output -> src/output.js, and db/frame-codec -> src/frame-codec.js). src/loadInputEffect.js and src/loadOutputsEffect.js are SQLite-only (non-Postgres-aware) duplicates of snapshot.js's functions; no importer of @smithers-orchestrator/db/loadInputEffect or /loadOutputsEffect exists. src/internal-schema/index.js is imported only by one test. The crucial risk: these forks are NOT Postgres-aware, so any caller that reached them would silently lose Postgres support.
recommendation: Remove the duplicate directory/standalone files (keep only the per-table internal-schema/*.js files that internal-schema.js / schema-migrations.js actually re-export: smithersScorers, smithersMemory*, smithersWorkspaceStates, smithersWorkspaceCheckpoints, smithersSchemaMigrations). Consolidate on the single canonical file per module to eliminate drift.
vnote: Consumers import @smithers-orchestrator/db/output (no slash) which resolves via the ./* export to src/output.js (the canonical, Postgres-aware file using isPostgresDb from snapshot.js); nobody imports any db/output/* or db/frame-codec/* subpath (grep returns 0). The output/ dir files are SQLite-only (e.g. output/selectOutputRowEffect.js uses plain db.select() with no POSTGRES branch), confirming the drift/Postgres-loss risk. Standalone src/loadInputEffect.js and src/loadOutputsEffect.js export loadInput/loadOutputs (SQLite-only) and have ZERO importers; the live, Postgres-aware loadInputEffect/loadOutputsEffect live in snapshot.js (exported via index.js). internal-schema/index.js is imported only by db-internal-schema.test.js:5 (a test that exists to compare the modular copies against canonical). Nuance worth noting: internal-schema.js DOES legitimately re-export smithersScorers/smithersMemory*/smithersWorkspaceStates/smithersWorkspaceCheckpoints from the dir, and schema-migrations.js imports smithersSchemaMigrations from it — the finding's recommendation correctly carves those live per-table files out. The duplicate per-table files for tables already defined inline in internal-schema.js (smithersFrames/smithersNodeDiffs/smithersTimeTravelAudit etc.) and the index.js barrel are the dead duplicates.

title: SmithersDb adapter's Postgres dialect branches are never tested
evidence: Postgres-only SQL lives in adapter methods: claimRunForResume (adapter.js:993-1017 uses IS NOT DISTINCT FROM + RETURNING), updateClaimedRun (1062-1073), deleteOutputRow (1184-1190), getRawNodeOutput (1274-1278), getRawNodeOutputForIteration (1317-1321), hasPhysicalTable (1304-1307 information_schema), upsertOutputRow (1156-1157). All tests that do `new SmithersDb(...)` use bun:sqlite in-memory only; the only Postgres test (tests/db-postgres-dialect.test.js) drives the lower-level SqlMessageStorage directly, never the SmithersDb wrapper. So these dialect branches have no coverage.
recommendation: Add a PGlite-backed SmithersDb test (mirroring db-postgres-dialect.test.js's setup) that exercises claimRunForResume/updateClaimedRun/deleteOutputRow/getRawNodeOutput/hasPhysicalTable through the adapter, asserting the RETURNING/IS NOT DISTINCT FROM/information_schema paths.
vnote: Verified claimRunForResume (adapter.js:993-1017 IS NOT DISTINCT FROM + RETURNING), updateClaimedRun (1062-1073), deleteOutputRow (1184-1190), getRawNodeOutput (1274-1278), upsertOutputRow (1156-1157) all gate on `this.internalStorage.dialect === POSTGRES`. The only PGlite/Postgres test in the package is db-postgres-dialect.test.js, which instantiates SqlMessageStorage directly (line 52) and never SmithersDb. Every test that does `new SmithersDb(...)` (db-adapter, db-getRawNodeOutput, db-transaction, db-concurrent, nodeDiffCache, migrations, alert-instances, db-adapter-sandbox) uses bun:sqlite (:memory:). So the SmithersDb Postgres branches have no coverage.

title: Multiple production-reachable adapter methods have zero tests repo-wide
evidence: Repo-wide test grep (.method() in packages/*/tests, apps/*/tests, e2e) returns 0 for: clearRunHijack (called apps/cli/src/index.js:3868), updateClaimedRun (called packages/engine/src/engine.js:4560), deleteOutputRow/deleteOutputRowEffect (called packages/time-travel/src/timetravel.js:210, retry-task.js:176), listApprovalHistoryForNode (called packages/engine/src/effect/deferred-state-bridge.js:246), listCacheByNode/Effect (called apps/cli/src/node-detail.js:444), hasPhysicalTable, listAllInProgressAttempts. listRunAncestry/requestRunHijack have coverage only elsewhere, not in packages/db.
recommendation: Add in-package adapter tests for these. deleteOutputRow especially needs coverage: its SQLite branch (adapter.js:1192-1254) has nontrivial PRAGMA-based column discovery + schema-fallback logic and snake/camel column-name resolution that is entirely unexercised.
vnote: Confirmed production call sites: clearRunHijack (apps/cli/src/index.js:3868), updateClaimedRun (engine.js:4560), deleteOutputRow (time-travel/timetravel.js:210), deleteOutputRowEffect (time-travel/retry-task.js:176), listApprovalHistoryForNode (deferred-state-bridge.js:246), listCacheByNodeEffect (apps/cli/src/node-detail.js:444), hasPhysicalTable (apps/cli/src/output.js:149), listAllInProgressAttempts (distinct from the tested listInProgressAttempts; defined adapter.js:1431). No test calls these on a real adapter. Minor wording nuance: listApprovalHistoryForNode and listCacheByNode/Effect DO appear in OTHER packages' tests but only as fabricated stubs (engine deferred-state-bridge-internals.test.js:132; apps/cli node-detail-unit.test.js:111), so the real adapter implementations remain untested — substance of the finding holds. deleteOutputRow's SQLite branch (adapter.js:1192-1254: PRAGMA table_info column discovery, schema-candidate fallback, snake/camel run_id/node_id/iteration resolution) is entirely unexercised. (getRawNodeOutput, correctly NOT in the finding's list, IS tested in db-getRawNodeOutput.test.js.)

title: Human-request adapter surface untested within packages/db
evidence: insertHumanRequest/getHumanRequest/answerHumanRequest/cancelHumanRequest/reopenHumanRequest/expireStaleHumanRequests/listPendingHumanRequests (adapter.js:1612-1708) and buildHumanRequestId have no references in packages/db/tests. They do have cross-package integration coverage (packages/engine/tests/human-requests.test.js, apps/cli/tests/ask-human-mcp.test.js), so behavior is exercised, but the package's own suite skips state-machine edge cases (e.g. answerHumanRequest only updates status='pending' rows; reopen only status='answered'; expire only pending+timeout<=now).
recommendation: Add focused adapter tests for the status-guarded transitions (answer/cancel only from pending, reopen only from answered, expire only timed-out pending, listPendingHumanRequests triggering expireStaleHumanRequests with the LEFT JOIN enrichment).

title: answerHumanRequest argument order differs between SmithersDb and the (dead) InMemoryStorage
evidence: SmithersDb.answerHumanRequest(requestId, responseJson, answeredAtMs, answeredBy) (adapter.js:1692) vs InMemoryStorage answerHumanRequest: (requestId, responseJson, answeredBy = null, answeredAtMs = Date.now()) (storage/InMemoryStorage.js:348) — params 3 and 4 are swapped. The StorageServiceShape.ts contract these two are supposed to share would be violated. Currently latent only because the storage module is dead, but it demonstrates the two implementations have already diverged.
recommendation: If the storage module is kept, align the signature with the real adapter; otherwise delete it (see dead-code finding). Either way, this confirms the duplicate is unmaintained.
vnote: SmithersDb.answerHumanRequest(requestId, responseJson, answeredAtMs, answeredBy) at adapter.js:1692 vs InMemoryStorage answerHumanRequest(requestId, responseJson, answeredBy=null, answeredAtMs=Date.now()) at storage/InMemoryStorage.js:348 — params 3/4 swapped. StorageServiceShape.ts:86 declares (requestId, responseJson, answeredBy?, answeredAtMs?), which InMemoryStorage matches but SmithersDb violates. Real callers (components/HumanTask.js:50, apps/cli/src/index.js:4289, engine human-requests.test.js:133) all use the SmithersDb order (answeredAtMs then answeredBy), so SmithersDb is the live/correct one and the shape contract + InMemoryStorage are the divergent dead ones. P2 appropriate (latent because storage is dead).

title: dialect.js exports isDialect and tableColumnsSql are never used
evidence: src/dialect.js:31 isDialect and src/dialect.js:188 tableColumnsSql have no importers anywhere (grep across packages/apps/.smithers/scripts excluding dialect.js returns only their definitions). dialect.js's own quoteIdentifier (line 39) is used only by the dead tableColumnsSql; sql-message-storage.js and schema-migrations.js each define their own local quoteIdentifier instead. The live dialect exports are POSTGRES/SQLITE/translatePlaceholders/translateDdl/columnType/beginTransactionSql/jsonExtractText.
recommendation: Remove isDialect and tableColumnsSql (and the now-orphaned quoteIdentifier) from dialect.js, or fold the three quoteIdentifier copies into the dialect one.
vnote: isDialect (dialect.js:31) and tableColumnsSql (dialect.js:188): repo-wide grep returns only their definitions — zero importers. dialect.js's quoteIdentifier (line 39) is used solely by tableColumnsSql (line 196) within the file and is not exported; zodToCreateTableSQL.js:36, schema-migrations.js:140, and sql-message-storage.js:368 each define their own local quoteIdentifier. Removing tableColumnsSql orphans dialect.js's quoteIdentifier. Live dialect exports (POSTGRES/SQLITE/translatePlaceholders/translateDdl/columnType/beginTransactionSql/jsonExtractText) are genuinely used. Confirmed P2.

title: SmithersDb.buildEventHistoryWhere duplicates SqlMessageStorage logic but is SQLite-hardcoded and unused
evidence: adapter.js:2158-2177 buildEventHistoryWhere emits json_extract(payload_json, '$.nodeId') (SQLite-only). The adapter's listEventHistory/countEventHistory delegate to internalStorage.listEventHistory (sql-message-storage.js:863-882), which uses the dialect-aware jsonExtractText. grep shows SmithersDb.buildEventHistoryWhere is never called (only its definition and the duplicate in SqlMessageStorage). It is exposed on the public class surface but dead, and would silently produce broken SQL on Postgres if anyone called it.
recommendation: Delete SmithersDb.buildEventHistoryWhere (the SqlMessageStorage one is the live, dialect-correct path) or have it delegate to internalStorage.buildEventHistoryWhere.
vnote: adapter.js:2158-2177 buildEventHistoryWhere hardcodes json_extract(payload_json, '$.nodeId') (SQLite-only, line 2170). grep shows it is only ever defined, never called — the live duplicate is sql-message-storage.js:863-882 (called at 890 and 902) which uses dialect-aware jsonExtractText(this.dialect, 'payload_json', '$.nodeId') at line 875. The adapter's listEventHistory/countEventHistory (2183-2184) delegate to internalStorage.listEventHistory, bypassing the dead adapter method. The dead method is on the public class surface and would emit broken SQL on Postgres if called. Confirmed P2.

title: classifyRunRowStatus is a permanent no-op shim left in the hot path
evidence: adapter.js:341-343 classifyRunRowStatus(row) just returns row; its own JSDoc (lines 327-340) says 'remove it once they call deriveRunState directly'. It is wrapped around every row in getRun (906) and listRuns (965), adding a layer that does nothing. The real classification now lives in runState/deriveRunState.js.
recommendation: Remove classifyRunRowStatus and its call sites in getRun/listRuns; rely on deriveRunState at the read sites (computeRunStateFromRow) as the comment indicates.

title: Input-bounds validators (assertJsonPayloadWithinBounds, assertMaxJsonDepth, assertMaxBytes, etc.) and JSON-bounds error paths are untested in-package
evidence: src/input-bounds.js re-exports 8 assert helpers (assertMaxStringLength/assertOptionalStringMaxLength/assertOptionalArrayMaxLength/assertPositiveFiniteNumber/assertPositiveFiniteInteger/assertMaxBytes/assertMaxJsonDepth/assertJsonPayloadWithinBounds). None of input-bounds.js or its assert*.js files appear in any tests/ import (comm against tests shows them absent). These contain non-trivial edge logic: circular-reference detection (assertMaxJsonDepth/assertJsonPayloadWithinBounds via a seen-Set), bigint/function/symbol rejection, finite-number checks, and ArrayBuffer/byteLength handling. Only validateRunRow/validateAlertRow exercise a thin slice indirectly via adapter tests.
recommendation: Add a unit test file for the assert* helpers covering boundary (== max), over-limit, wrong-type, circular-reference, non-finite-number, and non-serializable (bigint/function/symbol/undefined) cases.

title: Frame-codec edge/error paths (parse version mismatch, invalid path ops) untested
evidence: src/frame-codec.js throws on: parseFrameDelta non-object / unsupported version / missing ops (lines 28-40), and applyFrameDelta's setAtPath/insertAtPath/removeAtPath/getParentAndKey throw 'Invalid ...path' for malformed deltas. tests/db-frame-codec.test.js exercises encode/apply round-trips but the throw branches (version mismatch, invalid insert path into a non-array, numeric segment into a non-array) are not asserted (grep of tests for 'Unsupported frame delta version'/'Invalid insert path' returns nothing).
recommendation: Add tests asserting parseFrameDelta rejects bad version/shape and applyFrameDelta throws on structurally-invalid ops, to lock in the error contract reconstructFrameXml relies on.

title: react-output.js stripAutoColumns is a third copy of the same function
evidence: stripAutoColumns is implemented in src/output.js:40, src/output/stripAutoColumns.js, and src/react-output.js:4 (three copies). react-output.js is consumed (packages/components/src/components/Signal.js imports @smithers-orchestrator/db/react-output) so it is live, but it exists solely to avoid pulling the Drizzle-heavy output.js into the React bundle. The output/stripAutoColumns.js copy is dead (part of the dead output/ dir).
recommendation: Acceptable to keep react-output.js as a bundle-split boundary, but document why, and delete the output/ directory copy. Consider having react-output re-export from a shared leaf module to keep a single source of truth.
vnote: Three byte-identical stripAutoColumns implementations: output.js:40 (canonical, exported via index.js), output/stripAutoColumns.js (dead — part of the unreferenced output/ dir), and react-output.js:4. react-output.js is live (imported by packages/components/src/components/Signal.js:3 via @smithers-orchestrator/db/react-output) and serves as a Drizzle-free bundle-split boundary. The output/stripAutoColumns.js copy is the dead one. Severity P2 / medium confidence appropriate — keeping react-output.js is defensible; the dead copy and lack of a single source of truth are the real issues.

title: Postgres branch of deleteOutputRow/upsertOutputRow/getRawNodeOutputForIteration in SmithersDb is unreachable from tests (subset detail of Postgres-coverage gap)
evidence: adapter.js:1184-1190 (deleteOutputRow PG: DELETE ... WHERE run_id/node_id/iteration), 1156-1157 (upsertOutputRow PG: internalStorage.upsert), and getRawNodeOutputForIteration's PG path all gate on dialect===POSTGRES. db-getRawNodeOutput.test.js only covers the SQLite branch (uses bun:sqlite). No PGlite-backed SmithersDb test exists, so these specific output-table PG branches are uncovered in addition to claimRunForResume/updateClaimedRun already flagged.
recommendation: (verifier-discovered)

title: GeminiAgent.createOutputInterpreter has zero direct tests (all event branches untested)
evidence: src/GeminiAgent.js lines 60-208 implement a stream-json interpreter handling init/MESSAGE (with delta accumulation)/TOOL_USE/TOOL_RESULT/ERROR/RESULT/onExit. grep across tests/ for GeminiAgent feeding interpreter lines (createOutputInterpreter, onStdoutLine, TOOL_USE, "RESULT", MESSAGE) returns nothing. The only Gemini tests are capability metadata (capability-registry.test.js) and a configDir env-dump that pipes `{text:...}` through a fake CLI (agent-config-dir.test.js:153-179) — which never exercises the JSON event parser. buildCommand branches (approvalMode vs --yolo, sandbox, allowedTools empty-string vs list, extensions, includeDirectories, jsonReminder, resume) are also untested.
recommendation: Add a gemini-support.test.js mirroring amp-support/opencode-support: call createOutputInterpreter() directly with synthetic init/MESSAGE(delta)/TOOL_USE/TOOL_RESULT/ERROR/RESULT lines and assert AgentCliEvents; and unit-test buildCommand arg shaping for each branch.
vnote: Verified GeminiAgent.js: createOutputInterpreter (lines 60-209) handles init/MESSAGE(delta)/TOOL_USE/TOOL_RESULT/ERROR/RESULT/onExit, and buildCommand (213-280) has the branch set described (approvalMode vs --yolo, sandbox, allowedTools empty-string vs list, extensions, includeDirectories, --resume, jsonReminder, outputFormat). Grepped all of packages/agents/tests/: the ONLY Gemini-specific tests are capability-registry.test.js (metadata via createGeminiCapabilityRegistry/new GeminiAgent) and agent-config-dir.test.js:153-179, which only asserts GEMINI_DIR/GEMINI_API_KEY env via a fake CLI piping {text:...} — it never feeds JSON event lines to the interpreter and never inspects buildCommand args. No gemini-support.test.js exists. By contrast amp-support.test.js:111 and antigravity-support.test.js:18 directly call createOutputInterpreter()/buildCommand(). GeminiAgent is still user-selectable in apps/cli/src/index.js (z.enum includes 'gemini' at lines 1372/2361/5279, constructed at 6210) despite the @deprecated JSDoc, so this is a live production path with no offline coverage. P1 is appropriate.

title: AntigravityAgent stream-json interpreter is untested and effectively dead in practice
evidence: src/AntigravityAgent.js implements a full createOutputInterpreter (init/MESSAGE/TOOL_USE/TOOL_RESULT/ERROR/RESULT, lines ~ up to 249), but buildCommand hardcodes `outputFormat: "text"` (line 298) and --output-format is an unsupported agy flag (asserted in cli-capabilities.test.js:49). Since agy runs in text mode, the JSON event branches never fire in real use; only onExit's completed event is reachable. No test feeds the interpreter any line (grep for AntigravityAgent + createOutputInterpreter/onStdoutLine returns nothing). antigravity-support.test.js only checks buildCommand args.
recommendation: Either drop the unreachable JSON event branches (agy is text-only) or, if a future agy adds stream-json, gate the interpreter on outputFormat and add tests. At minimum add interpreter unit tests so the behavior is pinned.

title: VibeAgent interpreter/buildCommand only covered by a skipped e2e suite
evidence: src/VibeAgent.js createOutputInterpreter + buildCommand are exercised solely by tests/vibe-agent-e2e.test.js, which is `describe.skipIf(!isVibeInstalled ...)` (lines 38-39). CI has no `vibe` CLI (per repo memory: CI is agent-free), so these run zero assertions in CI. Unlike OpenCode (which has BOTH a 1328-line unit support test AND e2e), Vibe has no offline unit test. capability-registry.test.js only checks its metadata.
recommendation: Add vibe-support.test.js with direct createOutputInterpreter() line-feeding (assistant role JSON lines, onExit success/failure) and buildCommand arg assertions (maxTurns/maxPrice/maxTokens/enabledTools/resume/continueSession), matching the amp/opencode pattern so coverage holds in CI.
vnote: Verified VibeAgent.js createOutputInterpreter (74-162) and buildCommand (167-213, with maxTurns/maxPrice/maxTokens/enabledTools/--resume/-c continueSession/--agent/--trust/--workdir branches). The only test exercising them is tests/vibe-agent-e2e.test.js, gated by describe.skipIf(!isVibeInstalled || !supportsVibeE2EFlags) (lines 38-39). CI is agent-free (confirmed via repo MEMORY.md), so this runs zero assertions in CI. Grepping tests/ for Vibe references outside the e2e file finds only capability-registry.test.js:262 (metadata). No offline vibe-support.test.js exists, unlike amp/opencode which have offline interpreter+buildCommand unit tests. P1 is appropriate.

title: CLI capability doctor failure branches are entirely untested
evidence: src/cli-capabilities/getCliAgentCapabilityDoctorReport.js has ~12 distinct issue-emitting branches in diagnoseCapabilityRegistry (registry-version, skills-install-mode-without-support, skills-listed-without-support, missing-skills-install-mode, ui-methods-without-support, missing-ui-methods, unsupported-mcp-with-scope, mcp-bootstrap-without-scope) and diagnoseSurfaceContract (unsupported-flag-emitted, surface-binary-mismatch, missing-surface-docs, missing-resume-emission). cli-capabilities.test.js only asserts the all-pass happy path (report.ok===true, issueCount===0). No test constructs a malformed registry/surface to assert the right issue code+severity is produced — i.e. the validator's actual purpose is unverified.
recommendation: Add tests passing hand-crafted malformed AgentCapabilityRegistry/surface entries through diagnoseCapabilityRegistry/diagnoseSurfaceContract (or the public report) and assert each issue code+severity.
vnote: Verified getCliAgentCapabilityDoctorReport.js: diagnoseCapabilityRegistry emits registry-version, skills-install-mode-without-support, skills-listed-without-support, missing-skills-install-mode, ui-methods-without-support, missing-ui-methods, unsupported-mcp-with-scope, mcp-bootstrap-without-scope; diagnoseSurfaceContract emits unsupported-flag-emitted, surface-binary-mismatch, missing-surface-docs, missing-resume-emission (12 issue codes). cli-capabilities.test.js (the only test) asserts only the all-pass path: report.ok===true and issueCount===0 (lines 30-39). No test constructs a malformed registry/surface to assert any issue code+severity fires, so the validator's negative path is unverified. Minor scope note on the recommendation: diagnoseCapabilityRegistry/diagnoseSurfaceContract are module-private (not exported from cli-capabilities/index.js), so tests must either go through the public getCliAgentCapabilityDoctorReport (the auditor's stated alternative) or the file must export the helpers. The finding itself is correct; P1 stands.

title: BaseCliAgent.stream() path is unused by the product and has no tests (buildStreamResult/emptyUsage/asyncIterableToStream)
evidence: BaseCliAgent.stream() (BaseCliAgent.js:1081) builds a StreamTextResult via buildStreamResult (line 403), which uses emptyUsage (359) and asyncIterableToStream (380). grep for `.stream(` on agents across packages/engine, apps/cli, .smithers finds only AnthropicAgent/OpenAIAgent calling `super.stream()` (the ai-SDK ToolLoopAgent.stream, not BaseCliAgent.stream). No test ever calls `.stream(` (grep of tests/ returns none). So this whole cluster is reachable only via a public method nothing invokes.
recommendation: Either add a stream() test (real bash CLI agent + assert textStream/fullStream/usage), or remove BaseCliAgent.stream/buildStreamResult/emptyUsage/asyncIterableToStream if the CLI streaming surface is intentionally not part of the product API.
vnote: Verified BaseCliAgent.js: stream() at 1081-1084 -> buildStreamResult (403) -> emptyUsage (359) + asyncIterableToStream (380). Repo-wide grep for .stream( on agents: only AnthropicAgent.js:52 and OpenAIAgent.js:66 call super.stream(), and both classes extend `ToolLoopAgent` from the `ai` SDK (not BaseCliAgent) — so that is the ai-SDK stream, unrelated. No BaseCliAgent subclass (ClaudeCode/Codex/Gemini/Kimi/etc.) is ever .stream()'d in packages, apps/cli, .smithers, engine, components, e2e, or docs. The engine invokes agents exclusively via .generate( (engine.js lines 3471/3752/3997). No test calls .stream( (grep of tests/ empty). buildStreamResult/emptyUsage/asyncIterableToStream are referenced only inside BaseCliAgent.js (the node-detail-unit.test.js emptyUsage is an unrelated local helper in a different package). base-cli-agent-streaming.test.js tests createAgentStdoutTextEmitter, not this cluster. The dead-code framing is defensible (effectively-unexercised public method + its 3 private helpers); P2 with medium confidence is appropriate.

title: Observability metric emission path has no test assertions
evidence: BaseCliAgent.runGenerateEffect records agentInvocationsTotal, agentRetriesTotal, agentDurationMs, agentErrorsTotal, agentTokensTotal via internal helpers resolveRetryHint (182), extractAgentTokenTotals (109), recordAgentTokenMetrics (156), taggedMetric (75), normalizeMetricTag (63), resolveAgentEngineTag (89), asFiniteTokenCount (100). grep of tests/ for each of these symbols returns 0 files. No test verifies retry-hint detection increments retries, that token totals/tags are computed correctly, or that errors increment agentErrorsTotal — the production observability contract is unverified.
recommendation: Add a test that runs a fake CLI agent with a retry option and a usage-bearing stdout, capturing the Effect Metric state (or via a metrics test harness) to assert invocation/retry/token/error metrics and their tags.

title: ./BaseCliAgent subpath export declares types target missing its runtime exports
evidence: package.json maps `./BaseCliAgent` `types` to `./src/index.d.ts`, but index.d.ts contains 0 declarations for the helpers actually exported at runtime from src/BaseCliAgent/index.js (verified grep: extractUsageFromOutput, runAgentPromise, runCommandEffect, runRpcCommandEffect, createAgentStdoutTextEmitter, normalizeTokenUsage, tryParseJson, extractPrompt, resolveTimeouts, combineNonEmpty, pushFlag, pushList, normalizeCodexConfig, toolKindFromName, createSyntheticIdGenerator all return 0 in index.d.ts). External JS consumers already import via this subpath (packages/engine/src/AgentTraceCollector.js, apps/observability/src/_traceEventNormalizers.js import normalizeTokenUsage/extractTextFromJsonValue from '@smithers-orchestrator/agents/BaseCliAgent'). A strict TS consumer of these subpath exports gets untyped/any. No per-subpath .d.ts files exist (ls confirms none for BaseCliAgent/agent-contract/diagnostics/capability-registry/cli-capabilities).
recommendation: Generate/ship a per-subpath index.d.ts (the tsup build emits dts) and point each subpath export's `types` at its own declaration, or include the helper signatures in the bundled index.d.ts so the declared types match the runtime surface.

title: extractTextFromJsonValue (widely-used recursive util) has a single test case
evidence: src/BaseCliAgent/extractTextFromJsonValue.js handles many branches: string, array join, record.text/content/output_text, nested content array, type===text+part recursion, and recursion through response/message/result/output/data/item. It is imported by ClaudeCode/Codex/Pi/Amp interpreters, runRpcCommandEffect, and the observability app. extract-text-from-json-value.test.js (16 lines) tests exactly one negative case (reasoning part.text not surfaced). The string/array/output_text/nested-content/field-recursion branches are untested at the unit level.
recommendation: Expand extract-text-from-json-value.test.js to cover each return branch and the precedence order (e.g. content array vs nested message/result), since several CLI interpreters depend on its exact behavior.

title: createMcpToolset include filter and callMcpTool error/structured-content branches untested
evidence: src/mcp/createMcpToolset.js supports an `include` allow-list (line: `if (options.include && !options.include.includes(...)) continue`) and callMcpTool returns `{error:true,message,status:'failed'}` when result.isError and prefers result.structuredContent over joined text. mcp-toolset.test.js (real server, good) covers exclude+namePrefix and happy-path execute, but the demo server (fixtures/demo-mcp-server.js) only exposes two success-only tools, so `include`, the isError error-object branch, and the structuredContent branch are never exercised.
recommendation: Add an erroring tool and a structuredContent-returning tool to demo-mcp-server.js and add tests asserting the include filter, the {error,...} shape, and structuredContent passthrough.

title: HermesAgent (public export) has no dedicated tests
evidence: HermesAgent is exported from index.js (line 46) and is a thin OpenAIAgent subclass that throws AGENT_CONFIG_INVALID when baseURL/HERMES_BASE_URL is missing and defaults nativeStructuredOutput=false (src/HermesAgent.js). grep of tests/ for HermesAgent/Hermes returns no files. The missing-baseURL throw, the HERMES_BASE_URL/HERMES_API_KEY env defaulting, and the nativeStructuredOutput=false default are all untested. The AgentLike type-test (__type-tests__/AgentLike.assignability.test-d.ts) also omits HermesAgent, OpenCodeAgent, and VibeAgent from its assignability assertions.
recommendation: Add a hermes-support.test.js asserting: throws without baseURL, reads HERMES_BASE_URL/HERMES_API_KEY, and disables native structured output by default; and add Hermes/OpenCode/Vibe to the AgentLike assignability type-test.

title: Aspects accumulator + tracking config are render-time plumbing that the engine discards (dead data path)
evidence: Aspects.js resolves `tracking` (merging parent) and threads an `accumulator` into AspectContextValue; Task.js buildAspectMeta copies `tracking` and `accumulator` into `__aspects`. But packages/graph/src/extract.js:252-256 `aspects()` normalizer explicitly drops them: its own doc says "the render-time accumulator and tracking flags are dropped (the engine keeps its own durable per-run accumulator and budgets enforce regardless of tracking)." grep confirms `__aspects.accumulator`/`__aspects.tracking` are read nowhere in engine/scheduler. So createAccumulator(), AspectAccumulator, the accumulator field, and the tracking merge in Aspects.js all compute values that are never consumed downstream.
recommendation: Either remove the accumulator/tracking plumbing from Aspects.js + buildAspectMeta + AspectContextValue (and createAccumulator/AspectAccumulator) since the engine ignores them, or wire tracking through to the engine so the TrackingConfig actually gates metric collection. Right now the TrackingConfig type's documented behavior ('Track token usage. Default: true') is a no-op.
vnote: Verified directly. Aspects.js:27-35 builds resolvedTracking and accumulator into the context value; Task.js buildAspectMeta (300-309) copies tracking+accumulator into __aspects. graph/extract.js aspects() (252-292) keeps ONLY tokenBudget/latencySlo and explicitly drops tracking+accumulator (doc at 254-256). Repo-wide grep: the only reader of __aspects.tracking/accumulator outside the components package is the components' own test (composite-coverage.test.jsx:633). Zero references to `tracking`/`TrackingConfig` anywhere in packages/engine or packages/scheduler. The engine keeps its OWN durable accumulator (engine/src/aspects/createBudgetTracker.js, used at engine.js:4751,6125) which records usage unconditionally, so TrackingConfig ('Track token usage. Default: true', TrackingConfig.ts:5) is a genuine no-op. createAccumulator/AspectAccumulator and the tracking merge compute values never consumed downstream. P2 is appropriate.

title: aspects/index.js barrel is exported but imported by nothing
evidence: aspects/index.js re-exports { AspectContext, createAccumulator }. The package main index.js (lines 59-62) only does `export * from ./components/index.js` plus markdown/renderMdx/zod utils — it does NOT re-export the aspects barrel, and the umbrella packages/smithers/src/index.js does not export AspectContext/createAccumulator either (grep returned nothing). The only reachable path is the package.json `./*` wildcard subpath, and a repo-wide grep for `components/aspects`/`aspects/index`/`aspects/AspectContext` finds zero code importers (only scripts/generate-llms.ts:71 referencing a docs .mdx). Aspects.js/Task.js reach AspectContext via the deep relative path ../aspects/AspectContext.js, not via this barrel.
recommendation: Delete aspects/index.js, or if AspectContext is meant to be public, re-export it from the package index and add a consumer/test that imports through the public entrypoint.
vnote: Verified. aspects/index.js:9 re-exports {AspectContext, createAccumulator}. The package main index.js (packages/components/src/index.js:59-62) only does `export * from ./components/index.js` plus markdownComponents/renderMdx/zodSchemaToJsonExample; it does NOT re-export the aspects barrel. The umbrella packages/smithers/src/index.js has zero AspectContext/createAccumulator/aspects references. Repo-wide grep across packages/apps/.smithers/scripts/docs/skills/workflows/e2e found NO importer resolving to aspects/index.js: Task.js:15, Aspects.js:6, and the test (composite-coverage.test.jsx:30) all import the deep path ../aspects/AspectContext.js, never the barrel. package.json exposes a `./*` -> ./src/*.js wildcard but nothing imports through it. scripts/generate-llms.ts:71 and docs.json:199 reference a docs aspects.mdx, not the barrel. Truly dead. P2 appropriate.

title: SuperSmithers collides all 4 task outputs onto one key when reportOutput is provided
evidence: readOutput = reportOutput ?? "super-smithers-read"; proposeOutput = reportOutput ?? "super-smithers-propose"; applyOutput = reportOutput ?? "super-smithers-apply"; finalOutput = reportOutput ?? "super-smithers-report". When the documented `reportOutput` prop IS passed, the read, propose, apply, and report tasks ALL write to that same output table. Reading the report via ctx.latest(reportOutput) without a nodeId becomes ambiguous/co-mingled with the intermediate read/propose/apply rows. The fallback (no reportOutput) correctly uses 4 distinct keys, so the bug only manifests in the documented usage. composite-coverage.test.jsx:550-580 passes reportOutput:'report_out' but only asserts task IDs, never the output routing, so the collision is untested.
recommendation: Only the final report task should use reportOutput; give read/propose/apply distinct internal keys (e.g. `${prefix}-read` regardless of reportOutput). Add a test asserting only the report task carries reportOutput.
vnote: Partly right; severity overstated, mechanism partly inaccurate. CONFIRMED: SuperSmithers.js:39,56,69,86 route read/propose/apply/report ALL to reportOutput when it is passed (the documented usage per docs/components/super-smithers.mdx:32 and SuperSmithersProps.ts:15 'Output schema for the intervention report' — singular). Contrast Supervisor.js which correctly takes 4 distinct output props (planOutput/workerOutput/reviewOutput/finalOutput). The collision IS untested: composite-coverage.test.jsx:567-580 passes reportOutput:'report_out' but asserts only task IDs and the apply computeFn, never output routing. REFUTED detail: the claim that ctx.latest(reportOutput) 'without a nodeId becomes ambiguous/co-mingled' is wrong — driver/SmithersCtx.js latest(table,nodeId) requires a nodeId and keys rows by nodeId via filterRowsByNodeId.js (which calls lookupNodeId.includes('@@'), so a missing nodeId throws, not silently co-mingles). The real defect is all four heterogeneous outputs (incl. apply's {applied:true}) being validated/stored under one schema-keyed table, contradicting the single-report contract. Genuine bug but practical read-back works when given the report nodeId; P1 -> P2.

title: SuperSmithers 'apply' task is a no-op stub that returns a literal and writes nothing
evidence: The apply task's __smithersComputeFn is `async () => { /* The actual file writes trigger the hot reload system. */ return { applied: true }; }` — it performs no file writes; the comment claims the prior agent task already wrote to disk. So the apply task only emits a constant. composite-coverage.test.jsx:580 asserts exactly `resolves.toEqual({ applied: true })`, pinning the stub behavior.
recommendation: Either implement the apply step (or remove it and depend the report directly on propose), or document clearly that apply is intentionally ceremonial. As-is it is misleading: a task named 'apply' that applies nothing.
vnote: Verified. SuperSmithers.js:76-81 __smithersComputeFn is `async () => { /* ...The actual file writes trigger the hot reload system. */ return { applied: true }; }` — no file writes; inline comment (77-79) claims the prior agent task already wrote files. composite-coverage.test.jsx:580 pins it: `resolves.toEqual({ applied: true })`. A task named 'apply' that applies nothing is misleading. P2 appropriate. Note also an internal doc contradiction (see new finding).

title: Inconsistent host-prop sanitization across structural components
file: packages/components/src/components/Branch.js:11, Sequence.js:8, Ralph.js:13, Workflow.js:9 vs Parallel.js:11-16, MergeQueue.js:11-16
evidence: Parallel.js and MergeQueue.js deliberately build a sanitized `next` object ({maxConcurrency, id}) — Parallel's comment: 'Align prop sanitization with other structural components'. But Branch.js does `React.createElement("smithers:branch", props, chosen)` spreading the full props (leaking the `if` boolean and the `then`/`else` React elements as host-element props), and Sequence.js, Ralph/Loop.js, and Workflow.js likewise forward all props verbatim. The graph extractor (extract.js) does not read if/then/else from smithers:branch, so it's harmless today, but the codebase contradicts its own stated convention.
recommendation: Pick one convention. Either sanitize all structural components to only the props the extractor reads (id, maxConcurrency, children), or drop the sanitization in Parallel/MergeQueue. The current split makes the 'align with other structural components' comment false.

title: Poller onTimeout='fail' runtime path (loop exhausts, run fails) is not e2e tested
evidence: Poller.js maps onTimeout 'fail' (the default) to Loop onMaxReached:'fail'. All three e2e poller tests (poller-component.test.jsx) pass onTimeout='return-last'; the satisfied condition is always eventually met. remaining-coverage.test.jsx:423-426 builds an exponential poller with check `() => ({satisfied:false})` but only asserts timeoutMs — it never runs the workflow to verify a never-satisfied poll actually fails the run under the default onTimeout. So the default failure semantics are unverified end-to-end.
recommendation: Add an e2e poller test where the check never satisfies and onTimeout is left default, asserting result.status === 'failed' (or the documented failure shape).

title: EscalationChain: default humanRequest fallback and error/failed branches of defaultEscalateIf are untested
evidence: Every escalation test (escalation-chain-gating.test.jsx, composite-coverage.test.jsx) passes an explicit humanRequest, so the `request = humanRequest ?? { title: 'Escalation requires human review', summary: ... }` default at lines 120-124 is never exercised. defaultEscalateIf has three escalation triggers (row.error truthy, row.failed===true, row.ok===false) but tests only drive the `ok===false` path (gating test line 175) — the error and failed branches are untested even though bun line-coverage shows 100% (the && short-circuits collapse them onto one executed line).
recommendation: Add cases: humanFallback without a humanRequest (assert the default title/summary appear in the fallback approval meta), and prior-level results { error: 'x' } and { failed: true } to cover both extra defaultEscalateIf branches.

title: markdown table/thead/tbody components have no exact-output assertions
evidence: markdownComponents defines table/thead/tbody, but markdown-components.test.jsx only asserts exact output for tr/th/td (lines 95-103); table/thead/tbody are only covered by the generic 'renders a valid element' loop in remaining-coverage.test.jsx:480-491. Note also the table renderer emits no header-separator row (|---|), so the produced 'markdown' table is not valid GFM — acceptable for prompt text, but untested and undocumented as best-effort.
recommendation: Add exact-output assertions for table/thead/tbody (and a multi-row table) so the prompt-markdown format is pinned, and document that table output is approximate (no separator row).

title: Reconciler/driver/devtools tests are co-located in components but exercise a different package
evidence: driver.test.js imports @smithers-orchestrator/react-reconciler/driver, reconciler.test.js imports @smithers-orchestrator/react-reconciler, devtools.test.js installs bippy and tests SmithersDevTools — all three test the react-reconciler package, not components. They live under packages/components/tests and inflate this package's apparent test surface while leaving reconciler internals' coverage attributed here.
recommendation: Move driver/reconciler/devtools tests to packages/react-reconciler/tests so coverage and ownership match the code under test.

title: tsconfig outDir/declaration cruft conflicts with tsup build target
evidence: tsconfig.json sets `noEmit:false`, `declaration:true`, `emitDeclarationOnly:true`, `outDir:"dist"`, but the actual dts build is done by tsup (outDir:"src", dts:{only:true}) emitting src/index.d.ts, and the typecheck script runs `tsc --noEmit` (overriding noEmit). The tsconfig emit settings are inert and point at a dist/ dir nothing uses, which is misleading.
recommendation: Drop the unused emit-related compilerOptions (or align them with tsup) so the build pipeline is single-sourced and not contradictory.

title: SuperSmithers JSDoc contradicts its own apply implementation about what writes files
evidence: The component-level JSDoc step 3 (line 18) states '(If not dryRun) Compute task writes modifications to disk', but the apply compute task's inline comment (lines 77-79) says the opposite: 'The compute function has access to the proposed modifications... The actual file writes trigger the hot reload system.' implying the preceding AGENT task wrote the files, and the computeFn merely returns {applied:true} (line 80). docs/components/super-smithers.mdx:39 ('read -> propose -> apply -> report') reinforces the misleading impression that 'apply' actually applies. The two comments describe mutually exclusive mechanisms for who writes to disk.
recommendation: (verifier-discovered)

title: VCS-tag write path (tagSnapshotVcs) is orphaned — the whole vcs-version read feature no-ops in production
evidence: grep across packages/apps/.smithers/scripts shows tagSnapshotVcs has ZERO callers outside packages/time-travel/{src barrel, tests}. The only writer of _smithers_vcs_tags is the CREATE TABLE in packages/db/src/sql-message-storage.js:289 — nothing ever INSERTs. The engine populates _smithers_snapshots.vcs_pointer (engine.js:5141 vcsPointer: runMetadata?.vcsRevision) but never calls tagSnapshotVcs. Consequently loadVcsTag/rerunAtRevision/resolveWorkflowAtRevision always find no tag, and CLI `smithers replay --restore-vcs` (apps/cli/src/index.js:5371) flows restoreVcs:true into replayFromCheckpointEffect.js:34, which calls rerunAtRevision → loadVcsTag → returns {restored:false} silently. The CLI even prints a conditional 'VCS state restored' line (index.js:5374) that can never fire.
recommendation: Either wire tagSnapshotVcs into the engine's frame-commit/snapshot path so _smithers_vcs_tags is populated (making restore-vcs functional), or remove the vcs-version write/read module and the CLI --restore-vcs flag as an unfinished feature. Do not ship a flag that is structurally a no-op.
vnote: Verified independently. grep across the entire repo shows the ONLY INSERT/upsert into _smithers_vcs_tags is inside tagSnapshotVcsEffect.js:36-45, and tagSnapshotVcs has zero callers outside the package barrel (src/index.js:39, vcs-version/index.js), index.d.ts, and tests/vcs-version.test.js. The engine writes vcs_pointer ONLY into the _smithers_snapshots table (engine.js:5141, snapshot/captureSnapshotEffect.js:48) — a different table — never _smithers_vcs_tags. captureSnapshotEffect contains no call to tagSnapshotVcs. Therefore replayFromCheckpointEffect.js:35 -> rerunAtRevisionEffect.js:15 (loadVcsTag) always finds no tag and returns {restored:false} silently, and the CLI 'VCS state restored' line (apps/cli/src/index.js:5374-5375) under restoreVcs:true can never fire. The feature is structurally a no-op. P1 accurate.

title: resolveWorkflowAtRevision has no production or internal consumer
evidence: grep for resolveWorkflowAtRevision returns only the barrel export (src/index.js:39, vcs-version/index.js), the definition, and one test (tests/vcs-version.test.js:48). It is not consumed by replay, timeline, the CLI, server, or engine. Unlike loadVcsTag/rerunAtRevision (used by replay's effect path), this export is genuinely unreferenced by any caller — and its happy path (workspaceAdd) cannot even be reached since no tag is ever written.
recommendation: Remove resolveWorkflowAtRevision (and its Effect) unless a concrete consumer is planned; if kept as public API, document its intended caller.
vnote: Verified. Repo-wide grep returns only: the barrel export (src/index.js:39, vcs-version/index.js:9,11,47), the type decl (index.d.ts), and one test (tests/vcs-version.test.js:45-51). No consumer in replay, timeline, CLI, server, or engine. By contrast rerunAtRevision and loadVcsTag ARE consumed by replayFromCheckpointEffect.js, so this finding correctly singles out resolveWorkflowAtRevision as genuinely unreferenced. Its happy path (workspaceAdd) is also unreachable since no _smithers_vcs_tags row is ever written. P2 accurate.

title: vcs-version success paths are untested (only the null/no-tag branches are exercised)
evidence: Every test asserts the empty path: loadVcsTag returns undefined, tagSnapshotVcs returns null (no jj repo), rerunAtRevision returns {restored:false}, resolveWorkflowAtRevision returns null. There is no test where a tag exists and tagSnapshotVcs writes a row, where rerunAtRevision actually reverts, or where resolveWorkflowAtRevision creates a workspace. The branches in tagSnapshotVcsEffect.js:24-56 (jjOperationId capture, upsert, return tag), rerunAtRevisionEffect.js:19-23 (success), and resolveWorkflowAtRevisionEffect.js:23-30 (workspaceAdd success + VCS_WORKSPACE_CREATE_FAILED) are entirely uncovered.
recommendation: Add tests that seed a _smithers_vcs_tags row (via direct insert) and assert loadVcsTag returns it, rerunAtRevision invokes revert with the right pointer/root, and the VCS_WORKSPACE_CREATE_FAILED failure branch. Use a stub jj cwd or seed rows directly to avoid needing a live jj.
vnote: Confirmed at the time-travel package level: tests/vcs-version.test.js asserts only the empty paths (loadVcsTag undefined, tagSnapshotVcs null for no jj repo, rerunAtRevision restored:false, resolveWorkflowAtRevision null). The DB upsert branch in tagSnapshotVcsEffect.js:24-56, the success branch in rerunAtRevisionEffect.js:19-23, and the VCS_WORKSPACE_CREATE_FAILED branch in resolveWorkflowAtRevisionEffect.js:23-30 are uncovered. Note: the underlying jj primitives (revertToJjPointer, workspaceAdd, getJjPointer) ARE tested with real jj repos in packages/vcs/tests/jj-real-repo.test.js (gated by describeIfJj), so the gap is specifically the time-travel orchestration/DB logic, not the jj layer. Lowering to P2 because these branches are also unreachable in production (coupled to the orphaned-write-path finding) — adding success-path tests is good hygiene but not a correctness risk for shipped behavior.

title: revertToAttempt success + frame-cleanup path is not asserted
evidence: revert.js lines 53-66 (after a successful jj revert) find the last frame before the attempt's startedAtMs and call adapter.deleteFramesAfter. The 'emits RevertStarted and RevertFinished events on success' test only asserts events fire; it does not seed frames nor assert deleteFramesAfter ran, and in a CI box with no jj the revertToJjPointer call returns success:false, so the entire success branch (lines 50-67) is never executed. The result.success===true return and the frame-cleanup loop are untested.
recommendation: Inject a revert implementation (or use a real jj fixture) so result.success is true, seed frames straddling attempt.startedAtMs, and assert frames after the cutoff are deleted and {success:true, jjPointer} is returned.

title: timeTravel restoreVcs success path is jj-gated and weakly asserted
evidence: The only restoreVcs:true test is wrapped in jjTimeTravelTest = hasJjBinary() ? test : test.skip (line 14), so it is skipped in CI (no jj). Even when run, it asserts only `typeof travel.vcsRestored).toBe('boolean')` and `typeof travel.success).toBe('boolean')` — both pass whether restore succeeds or fails. timetravel.js:143-165 (the vcs-restore-failed early-return with TimeTravelFinished error event) is never exercised; the success branch is never strictly asserted.
recommendation: Add a non-jj test that drives timeTravel with restoreVcs:true against an attempt whose jjPointer revert is injected to fail, asserting success:false + error + the TimeTravelFinished failure event; and a success injection asserting vcsRestored===true.

title: JUMP_RUN_ID_PATTERN / JUMP_MAX_FRAME_NO exported from subpath but absent from main barrel and index.d.ts
evidence: jumpToFrame.js re-exports JUMP_RUN_ID_PATTERN and JUMP_MAX_FRAME_NO (lines 11-12 import, 21-22 export). The main barrel src/index.js:46 only re-exports jumpToFrame/JumpToFrameError/validateJumpRunId/validateJumpFrameNo from jumpToFrame.js — not the two constants. index.d.ts has no declaration for either constant. Because package.json maps every subpath's 'types' to ./src/index.d.ts, a TS consumer of @smithers-orchestrator/time-travel/jumpToFrame cannot see types for these runtime exports, and they are unreachable from the package root. They also have zero external consumers.
recommendation: Either drop the two re-exports from jumpToFrame.js (they are validated internally via validateJumpRunId/validateJumpFrameNo) or add them to the main barrel and to index.d.ts so runtime exports and types stay consistent.

title: diffSnapshots ignores outputTable changes in node-change detection
evidence: Node change detection compares only state, lastAttempt, and label: `if (aNode.state !== bNode.state || aNode.lastAttempt !== bNode.lastAttempt || aNode.label !== bNode.label)`. NodeSnapshot.outputTable (NodeSnapshot.ts) is part of the node record but a change to outputTable between two snapshots is silently not reported as a nodesChanged entry. Also formatDiffForTui (diff.js:150) renders nodesChanged purely as `from.state -> to.state`, so a node changed only by lastAttempt/label prints an identical 'state -> state' line.
recommendation: Decide intent: if outputTable can change, include it in the comparison; either way, make the TUI render reflect which field changed (e.g., show lastAttempt/label deltas) so a 'changed' node never prints an identical state arrow.
vnote: Partly refuted, partly confirmed. The outputTable claim is weak: outputTable (NodeSnapshot.ts:6) is a stable per-node output-table NAME derived from desc.outputTableName (engine.js:176,192), not output content; it does not realistically change for a fixed node+iteration key (iteration is part of the node key 'nodeId::iteration', so a different iteration is an added/removed node, not a 'changed' one), and actual output content is tracked separately via the outputs map (diff.js:62-70). Including it would add little. The CONFIRMED sub-issue is rendering: formatDiffForTui (diff.js:150) prints nodesChanged purely as 'from.state -> to.state', so a node changed only by lastAttempt/label (same state) prints an identical 'state -> state' arrow — reachable because diff.js:40 pushes lastAttempt-only deltas to nodesChanged. However formatDiffForTui has NO production caller (grep: only diff.test.js and e2e.test.js; the gateway returns the raw SnapshotDiff via diffRawSnapshots over RPC, and `smithers diff` uses a separate DiffBundle path), so this is cosmetic and library-only. P2 stands but the scope is narrower than stated.

title: recoverInProgressRewindAudits updateRun fallback branches are untested
evidence: The function has a try/catch fallback: if updateRun(status:'needs_attention') throws (older status enums) it retries with status:'failed', and if that also throws (run row deleted) it swallows. tests/recoverInProgressRewindAudits.test.ts only covers the happy path (status flips to needs_attention) and the empty no-op path. The needs_attention→failed fallback (lines 52-64) and the run-deleted swallow (lines 65-67) are never exercised.
recommendation: Add a test with an adapter whose updateRun rejects on 'needs_attention' to assert the 'failed' fallback payload, and one where the run row is absent to assert the audit row is still flipped to partial without throwing.

title: Rate-limit and audit-write helpers have no direct unit tests
evidence: grep of tests/ shows no test imports evaluateRewindRateLimit, updateRewindAuditRow, or countRecentRewindAuditRows directly; they are only exercised indirectly via jumpToFrame. Branch-level behavior is therefore untested: evaluateRewindRateLimit's maxPerWindow/windowMs override + Math.max(1,...) clamping and the remaining/windowStartedAtMs math; updateRewindAuditRow's two SQL variants (with vs without fromFrameNo, lines 14-34); countRecentRewindAuditRows' exclusion of in_progress rows (the result <> 'in_progress' clause).
recommendation: Add focused unit tests: evaluateRewindRateLimit with explicit max/window overrides and boundary counts (used===max → limited), updateRewindAuditRow both with and without fromFrameNo, and countRecentRewindAuditRows asserting in_progress rows and out-of-window rows are excluded.

title: expandResetSet exact-key fallback branch is uncovered
evidence: expandResetSet first matches by base nodeId (splitting 'nodeId::iteration'); only if that yields nothing does it fall back to exact-key match (lines 19-27, `if (result.size === 0) { for (const id of resetNodeIds) if (nodes[id]) result.add(id) }`). Fork tests (tests/fork.test.js:81, tests/e2e.test.js:71) pass resetNodes:['implement'] which always hits the base-id branch, so the exact-key fallback (passing e.g. 'implement::0') is never executed.
recommendation: Add a fork test passing a fully-qualified key (nodeId::iteration) in resetNodes to exercise the exact-key fallback and assert that node is reset to pending.

title: formatDiffAsJson is an identity-spread export with no production caller
evidence: formatDiffAsJson(diff) just returns { ...diff } (a shallow copy). grep shows zero production callers (gateway uses diffRawSnapshots only, gateway.js:31); it appears only in diff.test.js and e2e.test.js. It adds public-API surface for a no-op transform.
recommendation: Drop formatDiffAsJson (callers can pass the diff object directly) or, if it exists for symmetry with formatDiffForTui, document that it is an identity passthrough so consumers don't assume normalization.
vnote: Verified. diff.js:206-208 returns { ...diff } (shallow copy). Repo-wide grep shows zero production callers: gateway.js:4420 uses diffRawSnapshots and returns the raw object over RPC (no JSON formatter needed since SnapshotDiff is already serializable); apps/cli `smithers diff` uses apps/cli/src/diff.js (DiffBundle), not this. Only references are the barrel export (src/index.js:36), index.d.ts, tests/diff.test.js, and tests/e2e.test.js. P2 accurate; it is unused public-API surface for a no-op transform.

title: rerunAtRevision/replayFromCheckpoint error surface (vcsError) is captured but never shown to the user in the CLI
evidence: replayFromCheckpointEffect returns vcsError (from rerunAtRevisionEffect.js:21 result.error when a jj revert fails), but the CLI replay command only prints a success line when result.vcsRestored is true (apps/cli/src/index.js:5374-5375).
