CI's only test gate is the root `pnpm test` = `... && pnpm -r test`; `pnpm -r test` silently skips any workspace package lacking a `test` script (pnpm default). CI never runs a top-level `bun test`. So these 4+ unit tests for the primary published package (`smithers-orchestrator`, the `.` export of the monorepo) execute on zero PRs.
recommendation: Add `"test": "bun test tests"` to packages/smithers/package.json so `pnpm -r test` picks it up. Then verify the suite is green and consider failing CI when a workspace package with test files has no test script.
vnote: Fully reproduced. packages/smithers/package.json scripts (lines 60-63) = {typecheck, build} only, NO `test` key. tests/ holds 4 runnable bun tests (tools-unit.test.js, create-unit.test.js, define-tool-durability.test.js, exit-listener-leak-unit.test.js) plus src/__type-tests__/task-fork-jsx.test.tsx and workflow-input.test-d.ts. I ran them directly: 23 pass, 0 fail, 182 expect() calls — real tests. CI's only test gate is ci.yml:59 `pnpm test` → root package.json:74 `... && pnpm -r test`; there is NO top-level `bun test` anywhere in .github/ or scripts. I verified `pnpm -r --filter smithers-orchestrator test` produces zero test output and exits 0 (pnpm silently skips packages with no test script). Every other package with test files has a test-script=yes; only smithers-orchestrator (and the jj-* binary stubs, which have 0 tests) lacks one. So the flagship `.` export's tests execute on zero PRs. P0 stands.

title: No lint (oxlint) gate in any CI workflow
evidence: grep for `lint`/`oxlint` across .github/ returns NONE. Root package.json defines a full `lint` script (oxlint over packages/*/src + packages/*/tests) and `.smithers/package.json` defines its own `lint` (oxlint with .smithers/.oxlintrc.json), but neither ci.yml nor any workflow invokes them. oxlint is a devDependency (1.60.0) so it is installed but never run. The 'clean architecture / no dead code' bar relies on a linter that PRs never execute.
recommendation: Add a `pnpm lint` step to ci.yml's typecheck job (and `pnpm --filter ./.smithers lint`). Both are fast (oxlint), so they add negligible CI time.
vnote: Reproduced: grep -rniE 'lint|oxlint' .github/ returns NONE across all 4 workflows (ci.yml, faults.yml, faults-nightly.yml, pr-review.yml). Root package.json:68 defines `lint` (oxlint over packages/*/src+tests) and .smithers/package.json defines its own `lint`; oxlint@^1.60.0 is a root devDependency (line 155) so it's installed but never invoked by CI. ci.yml's typecheck job runs check:effect, check:deps, `pnpm -r typecheck`, and `pnpm --filter ./.smithers typecheck` — no lint. Nuance worth recording (does not lower severity): `pnpm lint` IS run by scripts/publish.mjs:162-163 during manual `pnpm release`, but that script is wired into NO workflow and is skippable via `--skip-checks`, so lint never gates a PR or main push. P1 stands.

title: No coverage measurement or gate anywhere in CI — the ~100% bar is unenforced
evidence: grep for coverage/--coverage/codecov/nyc/c8 across .github/ returns NONE; root bunfig.toml has no [test] coverage settings and root package.json `test` script passes no --coverage flag. Packages run `bun test tests` with no coverage. The stated bar is ~100% unit coverage incl. error/boundary cases, but CI neither measures nor enforces any coverage threshold, so regressions in coverage cannot fail a PR.
recommendation: Measure coverage in the test gate (bun test --coverage with a per-package or aggregate threshold) and fail CI below a target, or at minimum upload coverage so reviewers can see deltas on PRs.
vnote: Reproduced: grep -rniE 'coverage|codecov|--coverage|nyc|c8' .github/ and bunfig.toml returns NONE. bunfig.toml [test] block has only `root` and `preload`, no coverage settings. Root test script (package.json:74) passes no --coverage. grep for `coverage` across all in-scope package.json/toml files returns nothing. Packages run `bun test tests` with no coverage and no threshold. So the stated ~100% bar is neither measured nor enforced; coverage regressions cannot fail a PR. P1 stands.

title: typecheck:examples never runs in CI — 22 user-facing example workflows can ship broken
evidence: Root package.json defines `typecheck:examples: tsc -p examples/tsconfig.json --noEmit`. examples/ contains 22 .ts/.tsx reference files (e.g. examples/swe-evo/run.ts, examples/freestyle/workflow.tsx) and a dedicated examples/tsconfig.json that maps `smithers-orchestrator` to src. examples/ has NO package.json, so it is NOT a pnpm workspace package and `pnpm -r typecheck` skips it entirely. No workflow runs `typecheck:examples`. Examples are cited from docs (docs.json, index.mdx, integrations/tools.mdx) so broken examples reach users while CI stays green.
recommendation: Add `pnpm typecheck:examples` to ci.yml's typecheck job.
vnote: Reproduced. package.json:67 defines `typecheck:examples: tsc -p examples/tsconfig.json --noEmit`. examples/ contains 22 .ts/.tsx files and examples/tsconfig.json but NO package.json, so it is not a workspace package and `pnpm -r typecheck` skips it entirely. grep of .github/ for typecheck:examples/examples returns NONE. Examples currently typecheck clean (I ran `pnpm typecheck:examples`: exit 0), so nothing is broken right now, but no PR/main check protects them. Same nuance as the lint finding: `pnpm typecheck:examples` IS invoked by scripts/publish.mjs:168-169 at manual release time only, not in any workflow, and is skippable via --skip-checks. P1 stands.

title: Gateway OpenAPI drift check is gated only via faults.yml's `pnpm -r build`, not in the primary CI job
evidence: packages/gateway/package.json: `check:openapi` = `bun run scripts/generate-openapi.ts --check` and `build` = `bun run check:openapi && tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit`. generate-openapi.ts:255-261 exits 1 when packages/gateway/openapi.yaml is stale. ci.yml does NOT run `pnpm -r build`; only faults.yml:33 runs `pnpm -r build` (before the heavy e2e faults suite). So OpenAPI-spec drift detection (and every package's tsup --dts-only build validation) is enforced solely by the faults workflow. If faults.yml is ever made non-required, skipped, or flakes out on the e2e step, the OpenAPI drift gate disappears even though ci.yml stays green.
recommendation: Run `pnpm --filter @smithers-orchestrator/gateway check:openapi` (or `pnpm -r build`) in ci.yml so spec drift and dts-build failures are gated by the primary, always-required CI job rather than coupled to the e2e faults job.

title: jj platform packages' prepublishOnly binary-presence validation never runs on PRs
evidence: packages/jj-{darwin-arm64,darwin-x64,linux-arm64,linux-x64,win32-x64}/package.json each define `prepublishOnly` that throws if bin/jj (or jj.exe) is missing ('jj missing — run pnpm fetch:jj before releasing'). These run only on `npm/pnpm publish`, never in CI (no publish-dry-run or fetch:jj verification step in any workflow). A PR that breaks the jj fetch/packaging path or removes a binary passes CI green and only fails at release time.
recommendation: Add a lightweight CI step that runs `pnpm fetch:jj` (or at least asserts the platform binaries / postinstall+prepublishOnly invariants) so packaging breakage is caught on PRs, not at publish.

title: Full e2e suite runs in the test job without the build step that faults.yml deems necessary
evidence: e2e (@smithers-orchestrator/e2e, pnpm-workspace member) has `test: bun test` which runs the ENTIRE e2e tree (faults/ ~8800 lines, exports/programmatic-api.test.ts, budgets/). CI's `pnpm test` -> `pnpm -r test` therefore runs the full e2e suite in the lightweight `test` job WITHOUT a preceding `pnpm -r build` — yet faults.yml deliberately runs `pnpm -r build` before `test:faults`, implying built artifacts matter. This is both a likely duplicate execution of the faults suite (once unbuilt in ci.yml test, once built in faults.yml) and an inconsistency about whether a build is a precondition.
recommendation: Decide one home for the e2e suite: either exclude e2e from `pnpm -r test` (filter it out) and rely on faults.yml, or add the build step to the test job. Avoid running the heavy faults suite twice per PR under differing build conditions.

title: examples/ bun test (porting-rules.test.ts) never runs in CI — same untested-directory root cause as the smithers gap
evidence: examples/bun-port-smithers/components/porting-rules.test.ts is a real bun:test suite (imports describe/expect/test; 4+ test() cases asserting rsPathFor() mapping, e.g. line 14-15 expect(rsPathFor('src/http/http.zig')).toBe('src/http/lib.rs')). examples/ has NO package.json so `pnpm -r test` (the CI gate via ci.yml:59 -> package.json:74) skips it, and there is no top-level `bun test` in .github/. So this test executes on zero PRs even though it guards a documented reference example. Root cause is identical to the smithers-orchestrator finding: directories with test files but outside the pnpm-recursive test set silently go unrun.
recommendation: (verifier-discovered)

title: checkJs is off in all 27 packages: 831 .js source files (503 with JSDoc types) ship unverified types
evidence: Every package tsconfig sets `"allowJs": true` but omits `checkJs` (defaults false). Confirmed via grep across 27 tsconfigs (packages/*/tsconfig.json + apps/cli|observability|review) all `checkJs=ABSENT`. `npx tsc -p tsconfig.json --showConfig` in packages/errors confirms resolved config has `allowJs:true` and no checkJs. The per-package typecheck script is `tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` and CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml line 32: `pnpm -r typecheck`) runs it — so the bodies of `.js` files are never type-checked. There are 831 non-test/non-generated .js source files under packages/*/src + apps/cli|observability/src, of which 503 carry JSDoc `@param`/`@returns`/`@type`/`@template`. Example: packages/errors/src has 27 .js files densely JSDoc-annotated (index.js: 12 annotations) that emit the public d.ts but are not checked against their implementations. These packages author runtime code in .js with JSDoc *as the type source* — the one thing that makes that pattern safe (checkJs) is disabled repo-wide.
recommendation: Set `checkJs: true` repo-wide (add it to the shared compiler-options base that every package tsconfig should `extends` — note none currently extend a base, which is a related smell). Roll out package-by-package, fixing the JSDoc/implementation mismatches that surface, then gate it in CI via the existing `pnpm -r typecheck`. This converts 503 JSDoc-annotated .js files from 'documentation that silently lies' into checked types, and is the single highest-leverage type-safety policy fix for the published surface.
vnote: Independently reproduced. No tsconfig in packages/* or apps/{cli,observability,review} sets checkJs (grep across all 33 found zero), and `tsc --showConfig` in packages/errors resolves to allowJs:true with checkJs absent (default false). Counts are if anything understated: 833 non-test .js source files under packages/*/src + apps/{cli,observability}/src, of which 546 carry @param/@returns/@type/@template (finding said 831/503). typecheck script is `tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` (errors:24, agents:54); CI runs `pnpm -r typecheck` (.github/workflows/ci.yml:32). No package extends a shared base tsconfig (grep -l extends = 0), corroborating the related smell. I empirically proved the consequence: an isolated probe .js with JSDoc `@param {number} x` and a body `x.toUpperCase()` produced NO error under the repo's no-checkJs flags but TS2339 'Property toUpperCase does not exist on type number' WITH --checkJs. So JSDoc types are genuinely unenforced against .js bodies repo-wide. Severity P1 appropriate.

title: 28 packages commit a generated bundled src/index.d.ts that ships as exports `types` and can drift; drift guard exists only at publish, not in CI
evidence: 28 packages commit a `src/index.d.ts` (git ls-files 'packages/*/src/index.d.ts' + apps/cli + apps/observability = 28). Each package.json exports map points `"types": "./src/index.d.ts"` (verified in packages/agents and packages/errors), `files` ships `src/` (verified errors), and root .gitignore line 9 ignores `dist`. So the COMMITTED src/index.d.ts is what npm consumers consume, not a build artifact. The build script that produces it is `tsup --dts-only` (verified errors/agents package.json). scripts/publish.mjs lines 138-156 run `pnpm -r build` then a git-clean check 'the build regenerates committed declaration files ... If the build changes a tracked file ... this is exactly how 0.24.0 published a stale packages/smithers/src/index.d.ts' — proving drift has already shipped once. But .github/workflows/ci.yml has NO `pnpm -r build` + git-diff step (CI only runs check:effect, check:deps, typecheck, test). Concrete drift: packages/errors/src/index.d.ts last committed 2026-04-17 while its src/*.js last changed 2026-06-16 (two months newer). Pure-TS packages do this too (gateway-react, gateway-client, pi-plugin, protocol each commit a bundled src/index.d.ts).
recommendation: Pick one policy and enforce it in CI, not just at release: either (a) gitignore the generated `src/*.d.ts`, generate them at build/prepack, and stop committing them; or (b) keep them committed but add the publish.mjs drift guard to CI — a job that runs `pnpm -r build` and `git diff --exit-code` so a PR with a stale committed d.ts goes red. Today stale d.ts can merge to main between releases and only the release operator catches it (and 0.24.0 proves even that can slip).
vnote: Core architectural claim confirmed: `git ls-files` finds exactly 28 committed src/index.d.ts (packages/* + apps/cli + apps/observability); errors/agents package.json point exports `types` to ./src/index.d.ts and ship `files: [src/]`; root .gitignore:9 ignores dist; build is `tsup --dts-only`. The drift guard (pnpm -r build + gitStatusPorcelain check) exists ONLY in scripts/publish.mjs (lines 138-159, with a comment citing the 0.24.0 stale-d.ts ship), and CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) has NO build+git-diff step — verified the only run steps are install, check:effect, check:deps, pnpm -r typecheck, .smithers typecheck, and pnpm test. Pure-TS packages (gateway-react, gateway-client, pi-plugin, protocol) also commit bundled d.ts. ADJUSTMENT to the evidence (not severity): the 'concrete drift' sub-claim (errors d.ts committed 2026-04-17 vs src/*.js 2026-06-16) is misleading — a timestamp gap is not drift. I actually ran `tsup --dts-only` in packages/errors and it produced ZERO git diff, so that committed d.ts is in fact in sync; the newer .js commits did not change the public type surface. The systemic risk (no CI guard, drift has shipped once at 0.24.0) is real and P1 stands, but the cited example of current drift does not hold up.

title: isRecord / isObject / asRecord predicate family re-implemented across 7+ packages with divergent bodies; 99 inline error-message extractions
evidence: `isRecord` is defined independently in 7 distinct package roots: packages/agents (BaseCliAgent/parseHelpers.js), packages/components (DriftDetector.js), packages/db (frame-codec.js + frame-codec/parseFrameDelta.js + applyFrameDelta.js + encodeFrameDelta.js = 4 copies), packages/devtools (diffSnapshots.js), packages/errors (toTaggedErrorPayload.js), packages/pi-plugin (runtime/DevToolsClient.ts), apps/cli (why-diagnosis.js + workflow-pack.js). Bodies subtly diverge: agents uses `value != null && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value)`; components/db/devtools use `Boolean(value) && ...`; errors/cli use `Boolean(value && ... )`. A separate `isObject` family exists in packages/engine/optimization-artifact.js, packages/gateway-client (SmithersGatewayClient.ts, SmithersGatewayConnection.ts, sync/createSmithersGatewayTransport.ts), apps/cli/optimize-suite.js; plus `asRecord` x2 in gateway-client/sync. Beyond predicates, 99 inline `... instanceof Error ? ....message : String(err)` error-message extractions exist across packages/*/src + apps/cli/src (grep count). No shared low-level util package owns these; the divergent null-handling is a latent correctness drift risk (e.g. `value != null` vs `Boolean(value)` differ on `value=0`/`''` even though both then check `typeof === 'object'`).
recommendation: Add a tiny shared internal util (e.g. extend packages/errors or a new @smithers-orchestrator/std) exporting `isRecord(value): value is Record<string,unknown>` and `errorMessage(err): string`, and replace the 7+ isRecord / 4 isObject / 99 inline error-message sites. One canonical, tested predicate eliminates the body-divergence bug class and the duplicated test burden.

title: Published @smithers-orchestrator/observability lives under apps/ and forms a publish cycle with agents (depended on by 14 published packages)
evidence: apps/observability/package.json is `"name": "@smithers-orchestrator/observability"`, version 0.24.1, with NO `private` flag (so publish.mjs line 72 `if (!pkg.name || pkg.private) continue;` will publish it) and a full exports map shipping `src/`. It is depended on by 14 published workspace packages: db, engine, components, agents, driver, openapi, memory, time-travel, sandbox, server, scorers, vcs, smithers + apps/cli (grep of package.json deps), totaling 50 import sites under packages/*/src. It forms a TRUE cycle with agents: apps/observability/src/_traceEventNormalizers.js imports `@smithers-orchestrator/agents/BaseCliAgent` (extractTextFromJsonValue, normalizeTokenUsage) while packages/agents (ClaudeCodeAgent.js, BaseCliAgent/BaseCliAgent.js, runRpcCommandEffect.js) imports `@smithers-orchestrator/observability`. agents/package.json line 63 deps observability; observability/package.json line 42 deps agents. A core published runtime dependency physically residing under apps/ is a packaging surprise (apps/ is conventionally non-shipping), and the agents<->observability cycle hazards external consumers' install/resolution graphs and monorepo build ordering.
recommendation: Move observability to packages/observability so its location matches its shipped status, and break the cycle: extract the small agents-side helpers observability needs (extractTextFromJsonValue, normalizeTokenUsage) into a leaf package (or into observability itself) so the agents->observability edge is one-directional. This removes the only published cross-package import cycle and aligns the published surface with the packages/ convention used by every other shipped module.
vnote: Fully reproduced. apps/observability/package.json: name=@smithers-orchestrator/observability, version 0.24.1, no `private` flag; publish.mjs walks ['packages','apps','e2e','.smithers'] (line 60) and skips only `pkg.private` (line 72), so observability IS published. It ships src/ via files/exports (types->./src/index.d.ts, import->./src/index.js). 14 dependents confirmed (db, engine, components, agents, driver, openapi, memory, time-travel, sandbox, server, scorers, vcs, smithers + apps/cli), with 50 import sites under packages/*/src. The agents<->observability cycle is genuine and symbol-level, not just declared deps: apps/observability/src/_traceEventNormalizers.js imports extractTextFromJsonValue + normalizeTokenUsage from @smithers-orchestrator/agents/BaseCliAgent (both exported at agents/src/BaseCliAgent/index.js:24-25), while packages/agents/src/{ClaudeCodeAgent,BaseCliAgent/BaseCliAgent,BaseCliAgent/runRpcCommandEffect}.js import logWarning/logDebug/logInfo + metrics (agentDurationMs, toolOutputTruncatedTotal, etc.) from @smithers-orchestrator/observability. package.json edges confirm both directions (agents:63 deps observability; observability:42 deps agents). Verified observability's ONLY workspace dep is agents, so this is indeed the only cycle it participates in. apps/cli is the only other non-private app; all other apps (review/smithers/demos/studio) are private:true, so observability is the surprising case (a core runtime dep under apps/). Severity P1 appropriate.

title: apps/cli is also published from apps/ (non-private @smithers-orchestrator/cli) — apps/ contains 2 shipping packages, not the conventional zero
evidence: apps/cli/package.json has name=@smithers-orchestrator/cli with no `private` flag, so publish.mjs (scripts/publish.mjs:60 walks 'apps'; :72 skips only private) publishes it. Combined with apps/observability, two published npm packages live under apps/ while every other app (apps/review, apps/smithers, apps/smithers-demo, apps/smithers-studio-2, apps/smithers-tui-demo) is private:true (verified by reading their package.json `private` fields). This reinforces finding 3's location/convention smell: apps/ is treated as non-shipping by convention but is actually a publish root. A reader auditing 'the published surface' by looking at packages/* alone would miss two shipped packages, one of which (observability) is a transitive runtime dep of 13 packages. Suggested fix: either relocate cli+observability into packages/, or add an explicit allowlist of expected published package names in publish.mjs/CI so an app cannot accidentally go public by omitting `private`.
recommendation: (verifier-discovered)
